TextENGNew: One day in February, 1970...
``...Five minutes left.``
After verifying the time on his wristwatch, Yamaoki turned back... and checked the facial expressions of the riot police lined up there.
They stood in total silence... and every one of them had a sharp look in their eyes.

Their faces were hidden behind their protective helmets, but they were filled with anger and a sense of purpose toward their mission, while standing with their bulletproof shields overlapping and their batons in hand.
(All right...)
Upon seeing that, Yamaoki readied himself as well and turned forward once again.
Right before his eyes... was a privately owned resort facility, ``Shirakaba mountain villa.``
It was a slight building, named after the magnificent birch forest nearby, but the steep cliff-like slope of the mountain behind it gave it a pleasant view overlooking the distant Shinshu mountains.
changeReason: Old: 'It was a small building'; new: 'It was a slight building.' JPN 小さな建物 means small building; 'slight' is an incorrect word choice that changes the meaning.

TextENGNew: ...In other words, the besieged criminals could overlook the entire hillside where Yamaoki and his men were standing in wait, which is why even though they'd conducted two raids on the facility, the criminals successfully repelled both attacks.
(...The raid two days ago was absolutely terrible.)

They rushed in through the drainage canal, but the poor weather made their tear gas grenades unusable. And on top of that, they had no means to fight back because use of firearms was prohibited. The riot police captain in charge of the raid even died in the attack.

Thus, even though he was originally assigned to the rear guard, Second Division Captain Yamaoki became appointed as field commander since he had experience leading the riot police in the T University siege incident one year prior.
(Still, they probably only chose this mountain villa after escaping without even thinking about it... But even so, this is a tough one.)
A siege defense is the most effective tactic for dealing with a disadvantage in numbers.
And on top of that, the topography also presented disadvantages that made an attack difficult, so it would be very demanding to launch a raid without sacrifices.
And what's more, not only did they have hostages, but the assailants robbed a gun shop during their escape, so they were armed too.
...It was so cunning that even Yamaoki, who had dealt with similar situations before, could not help click his tongue in irritation.
Meanwhile, the first division squad members who experienced a fellow officer being killed were ready to beat the criminals to death if given permission.
Really, the fact that there was so little confusion even with a sudden change in command was evidence of the intense hostility they felt toward the criminal group.
(But the police mustn't act out of hatred.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2072. Otherwise they are no different from thugs.)
Plus... as a member of the Metropolitan Police Department and acting under the National Police Agency, protecting the hostages and apprehending the perpetrators alive was Yamaoki's strict duty.
And that was not just for humanitarian reasons.

...It was to prevent the criminals from being deified as ``martyrs`` who died standing up to authority, and keep other extremist groups from looking up to them and seeing them as a reason to expand their activities.

Additionally, people have a strong tendency to criticize police officers for shooting indiscriminately, so if one of the criminals was injured by gunfire the firing officer may be accused of criminal activity.
It is ironic, but the dignity of the police force suffers when they are accused of breaking the law.
...When told about it that way, Yamaoki had no choice but to obey.
Time went by as he pondered that to himself... and before long, the minute hand was pointing straight at 10 o'clock.
The next moment, Yamaoki was barking into the transceiver in hand with a small, but sharp voice.
``...Special vehicle team, commence the operation!!``
``Roger!!``
The moment he gave that signal, a crane truck cleverly hidden in a mountain recess emerged from the trees and began approaching the mountain villa.
And hanging from the tip of the crane's huge arm was a massive iron ball... better known as a wrecking ball.
A special vehicle typically used for demolishing buildings, that had been borrowed from a nearby construction company.
And, its role today was...
``Gwah?!``
``Gyaa?!``
A sudden roar echoed out and the entire building shook, as the criminals inside were violently shaken and fell to the ground.
``Was that an earthquake?!``
``No... that isn't it!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_teroA2003. This is... wha?!``
One man looked out through the barricaded window and groaned as soon as he realized the source of that quake.
What he saw before his eyes was... the shadow of a massive ball of iron, unlike anything he would ever seen before.
changeReason: New introduces grammatical error 'unlike anything he would ever seen before' (should be 'had ever seen'). Meaning unchanged but the error is notable.

TextENGNew: And it was letting out a colossal roar, while heading this way again...?!
``I-it's going to hit us?!``
Forgetting to even bother shooting back, the criminals panicked and fled the area.
The next moment... the barricade was blown away, with a sound like a bomb going off...
Thanks to the impact of that blow, two people were struck by the wall and fainted in agony.
``Y-you bastards...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_teroA2005. Do you not even care that there are hostages in here?!``
``Th...that isn't it!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_teroB2004. The police must know the hostages aren't in this part of the building...!``
...Right.

Thanks to intel a suicide corp found while investigating under the cover of darkness, Yamaoki already knew that the criminal group was stationed on the third floor, while the hostages were on the second.
Based on that, they obtained blueprints of the mountain villa and carefully examined them to determine the best locations and angles to use the wrecking ball.
The first location damaged was chosen to prevent them from using the window to launch a counterattack.
And, next...!
``Uwaaah!!``
Once again, the criminals staggered and fell to the ground from the intense vibrations, and gasped when they saw the destruction right before them.
What was destroyed this time... was the staircase leading to the second floor.
It became a total wreck, and it was no longer possible for even a single person to slip through there...!
``...Special vehicle corps, reporting in!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kidouA2002. We've successfully crushed the third-floor stairwell!``

``All right, begin the second phase!@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2076. All water cannon trucks, surround the villa on all sides!@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2077. Riot squad number four, prepare to fire tear gas!``
``Water cannons one through three are ready and standing by!``
``Squad four is in firing position!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kidouC2002. Awaiting your orders!``
``Squad four, commence tear gas bombardment!!@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2079. Squad three, rush in as soon as the volley is fired and secure the entrance barricade!! Gooo!!!``
````Yeaaaaaaaahhh!!!````
``Two offenders on the first floor have been apprehended after firing on us!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kidouA2005. One squad member was wounded, but injuries are minor!``
``This is squad three, we have secured the hostages!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kidouB2004. ...They're safe and alive!!``
``...Ah, aaaaahh...!!``
The landlord standing behind Yamaoki immediately broke down and started crying as soon as he heard that report.
One of the hostages was his wife.
It was still far too soon to rejoice, but that news alone was enough to put his heart at ease.
...Just then.
``..? Squad one, what happened?!@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2081. Maeno, what's going on?!``
...It was a moment of carelessness.
As squad two emerged from the slope of the mountainside to break the barrier on the first floor, squad one led by Maeno passed by them and used a ladder to invade the third floor.

And then, they found the room where the criminals were holed up, and the moment they all prepared themselves for a simultaneous assault...@k another one of the criminals, who just happened to be taking care of something else, emerged from behind them.
``U-uwaaaaahh!!``
The armed gunman screamed and pulled the trigger the moment he saw the riot police.
Maeno was commanding the group from behind, and regrettably, as he turned around... the bullet shot straight through the kneecap on his right leg.
``C-captain?!``
All strength drained from his leg, Maeno collapsed to the ground in agony.
The squad members in the back rushed to cover him with their bulletproof shields, but their movement was delayed because they turned their attention toward the noises coming from the room.
(Shit... this is it for me!)
Maeno watched as the criminal pointed the gun toward his head... then closed his eyes.
And then...!
A dry, explosive pop came from behind.
And following that... Maeno heard something falling to the floor in front of him, and opened his eyes again.
The criminal had fallen to the ground... and was not moving.
And, the source of the gunshot behind him...
``M...Minai-san?!``
``...Command car, this is squad two.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2276. We've taken control of the third floor.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2277. All but one of the perpetrators have been arrested.``
``All but one...?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2083. What happened to the other one?``
``...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2279.I'm sorry.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2280. One of the criminals had left the room to go on patrol, and came at us from behind.``
``He was aiming his sights at me, so one of my subordinates... Deputy Inspector Minai fired back.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2282. The target was shot and is currently in a state of cardiac arrest.``
Yamaoki could not help sigh after hearing that report.
...He did not lament his subordinate's mistake.
He cursed his own failure to come up with an appropriate strategy, which gave him no choice but to act accordingly.
(I knew it. I wish we would carried out a feint operation to keep all their attention on a single place...)
changeReason: New introduces grammatical error 'I wish we would carried out' (should be 'we'd carried out'). Meaning unchanged but the error is notable.

TextENGNew: Although... even if they carried out a feint operation, the leader of the attack would be in danger of getting sniped by the criminals.
Two agents had already lost their lives... and one more was still in a critical situation, so strong measures had to be taken to ensure there would be no more sacrifices...
``...Then, Maeno,@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2087. are you safe?``
``I'm sorry... I was hit in the knee.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2284. But my life isn't in danger.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2285. More importantly...``
``Right... I understand. No need to say anything more.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2089. Anyway, we'll have the rescue team sent in right away.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2090. ...Thank you for your service.``
Yamaoki cut off the transceiver after saying that.
And then, he watched the riot police escort the criminals this way, as the media flocked to take pictures.
(Help Minai, huh...?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2092. I already know that much.)
By now, they were probably already reporting to the TV staff that one of the perpetrators was shot by an officer.
...But unlike the T University incident, this time they had made various preparations with the interview team in advance.
Aside from the gossip magazines, the major newspapers and key TV stations should follow directions.
And in which case, what they had to do going forward was...
``Commander Yamaoki, Chief of Staff Yuki is calling.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_kidouA2008. He'd like to speak with you about public relations measures going forward.``
``Right... understood.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2094. I'll head straight over.``
Now, from this point on was the true opportunity to exercise his skills.
He could not let an exceptional detective be sacrificed just to appease humanitarians.
